question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the expireAfterAccess=60m policy in EntityId.CACHE to keep 500,000 entries alive for 60 minutes by periodically re-accessing them, maintaining maximum heap pressure on ≥30% of nodes for an extended period without the cache ever evicting entries?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with IDs that are valid three-part strings but have leading zeros (e.g., 00.00.01000), and if EntityId.of(String) parses them via Long.valueOf() which accepts leading zeros, cause unexpected cache key collisions or duplicate DB lookups that amplify load on ≥30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that EntityId.of(String) uses Splitter.on('.').omitEmptyStrings().trimResults() to parse IDs, and send IDs with Unicode whitespace characters that trimResults() does not trim, causing Long.valueOf() to throw NumberFormatException that propagates as a 400 error, and by sending millions of such requests, exhaust the error-handling thread pool on ≥30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with IDs that are valid but correspond to topics with very large metadata fields in the Topic/AbstractTopic entity, causing topicRepository.findById() to load large BLOBs or CLOBs into memory, and by sending many concurrent requests, exhaust the JVM heap on ≥30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with IDs that encode to values just above and below the shard=0, realm=0 boundary (where encode() returns num directly vs. the full bitwise encoding), causing the EntityId.CACHE to store entries with IDs that appear numerically close but represent completely different entities, leading to cache pollution that affects ≥30% of nodes?
